ddls:
CREATE TABLE "supercomputers" (
  "system_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supercomputer system */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the supercomputer system */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the supercomputer */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution where the supercomputer is located */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location of the institution */,
  "interconnect" TEXT /* Type of interconnect used in the supercomputer */,
  "cpu_model" TEXT /* Model of the CPU used in the supercomputer */,
  "cpu_frequency" REAL /* Frequency of the CPU in GHz */,
  "list_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the list the system is part of */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the system in the list */,
  "cores" INTEGER /* Number of cores in the system */,
  "rmax" REAL /* Actual performance of the system in TFlop/s */,
  "rpeak" REAL /* Peak performance of the system in TFlop/s */,
  "power" REAL /* Power consumption of the system in kW */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system used on the supercomputer */,
  "memory" REAL /* Total memory in gigabytes (GB) available in the system */,
  "storage" REAL /* Total storage capacity in terabytes (TB) available in the system */,
  "cooling_system" TEXT /* Type of cooling system used (e.g., air, liquid) */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Date when the system's data was last updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or remarks about the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_supercomputers_list_id FOREIGN KEY ("list_id") REFERENCES lists ("list_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lists" (
  "list_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each list */,
  "list_name" TEXT /* Name of the list (e.g., Top500, Green500) */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Date when the list was published */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the list */,
  "criteria" TEXT /* The criteria used to rank the systems in the list */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the official website or documentation for the list */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("list_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin, data analyst) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the user */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution or organization the user is associated with */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department within the institution */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "system_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supercomputer system being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, compare, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the system */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_system_id FOREIGN KEY ("system_id") REFERENCES supercomputers ("system_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
